title: I pronomi: suono, ritmo, cliticizzazione
pronunciation_features_introduced: open /ɛ/ in lei and open /ɔ/ in noi / voi (reusing the vocali-lesson distinction)
clitic phonology: object pronouns bind to their verb host without pause
gli as /ʎi/ (same palatal lateral from the consonanti lesson, now in a pronoun role)
no stress on clitic object pronouns; stress always falls on the verb
grammar_features_introduced: Italian subject pronouns (io, tu, lui, lei, noi, voi, loro) as pro-drop default
direct-object pronouns (mi, ti, lo, la, ci, vi, li, le) with clitic position before the verb
indirect-object pronouns (mi, ti, gli, le, ci, vi, gli/loro) parallel in phonology, different in function
homograph trap: lo / la / le as both article and direct-object pronoun, disambiguated by what follows
contemporary use of gli for 3pl indirect object in place of formal loro
forward references to pronomi riflessivi and pronomi tonici (future grammar lesson)
cognate_patterns_showcased: Italian pro-drop vs. French/English obligatory subject pronoun
Italian clitic pronouns parallel French le/la/les (Francophone instinct transfers)
Italian gli/le ↔ French lui; Italian 3pl gli ↔ French leur (slight distribution difference)
notes: Third A1 pronunciation lesson. Covers subject / DO / IO pronouns with individual drills for ≥3-char forms (lui, lei, noi, voi, loro, gli) and short-phrase drills for ≤2-char clitics (mi, ti, lo, la, ci, vi, li, le). The drill-constraint is explicitly mentioned to the reader as a pedagogically correct artifact of clitic phonology rather than a tool limitation. Reflexive and tonic pronouns are flagged for a future grammatica-base lesson.
